goal: Admin approves/rejects a workshop (persists with timestamp/reason); approved workshops show a certified badge on every workshop surface; pending/rejected visually distinguished.
why: Closes the trust loop M1 opened: registration captured workshop credentials but nothing made them real or visible. This phase makes verification a server-checked action and surfaces the result so an owner can see a job was done by a verified workshop.
success_criteria: Admin clicks Approve on a pending workshop -> status becomes verified, reviewed_at is set, row leaves the pending list without a full page reload (VERIF-03).
Admin clicks Reject, enters a reason, confirms -> status becomes rejected, rejection_reason stored, row leaves the list (VERIF-04).
A verified workshop shows the certified badge on the mechanic header and matching owner history surfaces; pending/rejected show distinct indicators (VERIF-05, VERIF-06).
All badge/button strings are bilingual (present in both el and en); no raw hex in any new or modified JSX.
A non-admin approveWorkshop/rejectWorkshop call returns forbidden and mutates no row (VERIF-05 criterion #5).

tasks.title: Admin approve/reject server actions + badge i18n keys
acceptance_criteria: approveWorkshop(id) sets status to verified and reviewed_at to now; returns { ok: true }.
rejectWorkshop(id, reason) sets status to rejected, stores reason in rejection_reason, sets reviewed_at; returns { ok: true }.
A caller whose app_metadata.role !== 'admin' (or unauthenticated) gets { ok: false, error: 'forbidden' } and no row is mutated.
An empty/whitespace reason to rejectWorkshop returns { ok: false, error: 'reason' } without a DB write.
Both el and en dictionaries contain all nine new keys; npx tsc --noEmit passes.
tasks.action: Create src/lib/admin-actions.ts ('use server'). Both approveWorkshop and rejectWorkshop call a getSessionRole() guard FIRST, returning { ok:false, error:'forbidden' } for non-admin. approveWorkshop updates { status:'verified', reviewed_at } by id. rejectWorkshop trims reason (empty->'reason'), updates { status:'rejected', rejection_reason, reviewed_at }. Both revalidatePath('/admin'). Add nine bilingual badge/button keys to the Translation type and both el and en dicts in i18n.ts.

tasks.title: WorkshopBadge component + verified-names helper
acceptance_criteria: WorkshopBadge status=verified renders a green chip with shield icon and t.certified; pending renders muted with t.pendingReview; rejected renders negative-toned with t.rejected.
The badge uses only semantic tokens (no raw hex, no default Tailwind palette).
The badge is bilingual via useLang().
getVerifiedWorkshopNames() returns a Set<string> of workshops.name where status = verified; returns an empty Set on error (never throws).
tasks.action: Create src/components/WorkshopBadge.tsx ('use client') exporting WorkshopBadge + type WorkshopStatus='verified'|'pending'|'rejected'. One inline chip via useLang()+shield Icon, status->tokens: verified bg-positive/10 text-positive, pending bg-surface-2 text-muted, rejected bg-negative/10 text-negative; labels t.certified/t.pendingReview/t.rejected. Create src/lib/workshop-status.ts ('server-only') getVerifiedWorkshopNames(): select name where status=verified, return a Set, empty on error.

tasks.title: Wire approve/reject into AdminReviewList; propagate badge to mechanic + owner surfaces
acceptance_criteria: Each pending admin row shows Approve and Reject buttons; Approve removes the row optimistically and calls approveWorkshop; status becomes verified in the DB.
Reject reveals an inline reason input + confirm; submitting removes the row optimistically and calls rejectWorkshop with the reason; rejection_reason is stored.
On /mechanic a signed-in verified workshop shows the green certified badge; pending shows muted Pending; rejected shows negative Rejected; anonymous use falls back to the existing hardcoded header.
On / the vehicle hero badge reflects whether the latest service place matches a verified workshop, and each history entry whose place matches a verified workshop shows the certified badge.
npx tsc --noEmit passes; no full-page reload on approve/reject.
tasks.action: AdminReviewList: useOptimistic removes rows by id; per-row Approve/Reject call approveWorkshop/rejectWorkshop in startTransition; Reject shows an inline reason input; strings via t.*. MechanicLog: add workshopStatus?: WorkshopStatus prop, render <WorkshopBadge> else keep fallback. mechanic/page.tsx: fetch own status via from('workshops').select('status').maybeSingle(). ServiceLog: add verifiedWorkshops prop for the hero badge + per-entry place match. page.tsx: getVerifiedWorkshopNames().
